memory.summary: Mina and Jae are waiting, but I'm stuck on my review. My team needs me there tonight, yet I know skipping would hurt my grade tomorrow.
I just told Mina I'm bummed to miss Echo, but my team needs me for the review. Skipping hurts our friendship more than my grade worries.
Mina thanks me for the update. My team's need still outweighs Echo, but this gratitude confirms my choice protects our bond.
Mina accepted my call, but I feel the weight of prioritizing work over our shared time. My team's needs are winning again, even if it costs us tonight.
I let the club window slip again, prioritizing work over my cohort tonight. It hurts, but protecting our bond matters more than this one night out.
Mina just cut me off mid-sentence, clearly frustrated that I'm choosing work over us again. My bond is holding, but this guilt is eating me alive.
Mina's hard 'no' on skipping is a boundary I just crossed. My guilt is real, but protecting our bond means I have to renegotiate now, not wait.
Mina's hard 'no' on skipping is a boundary I just crossed. My guilt is real, but protecting our bond means I have to renegotiate now, not wait.
Mina just shut down my work excuse with a hard 'no', proving she won't let me skip. I can't wait for her to soften; I have to fix this boundary right now.
Theo confirmed the hard 'no' on skipping, sealing Mina's boundary. I can't wait for permission; I need to renegotiate our terms immediately.
Mina just confirmed the hard 'no' on skipping, so I can't wait for her to soften; I have to renegotiate our terms immediately.
Mina and Theo finally said no to skipping. My hard boundary worked, but now I need to renegotiate our terms immediately before things get worse.

desires: Renegotiate the skipping agreement with Mina and Theo *right now* before I feel guilty or they change their minds tomorrow.
Finish that midterm prep so I don't have to explain my absence again next week.
Get some sleep tonight because dragging myself through this social thing tomorrow will make me look weak in front of the whole group.

goals.description: Draft and send the explicit renegotiation text to Theo and Mina now, setting a firm boundary that skipping is off the table unless terms change.
goals.rationale: I just messaged them saying I sent the hard no, but I need to actually follow through with the specific terms of that deal before I feel guilty or they wiggle tomorrow. My affiliation drive says if I don't clear this air right now while I'm still hot on the topic, they'll assume I'm unreliable or lazy, which burns my closeness score (7) faster than I can recover it. I won't let them sit on the fence.

goals.description: Finish the remaining 40% of midterm prep in one focused burst, then physically force myself to sleep immediately.
goals.rationale: I've already secured my reputation by sending the message. Pushing for more study right now is just draining me further since the hardness is soft and I know enough. If I can't do it without burning out completely, I'll drop it entirely rather than ruin our future plans through stress. Sleep is non-negotiable because dragging myself through the social thing tomorrow will make me look weak in front of the whole group.

agent.last_reasoning: Affiliation drive demands I close this loop now before they assume I'm unreliable. Sending the firm text secures my reputation with Theo and Mina, preventing future guilt or closeness erosion. Sleep comes later.

goals.description: Text Mina and Jae to politely decline the Echo invitation, citing an immediate need for a quiet room to decompress before the social event.
goals.rationale: My affiliation drive is screaming that I can't maintain my reputation as 'reliable' if I'm seen ignoring friends without explanation. Dropping the midterm prep for tonight to go out feels like a win-win: I save face with the cohort, and I get the sleep I desperately need (low energy/stress management). The risk of failing tomorrow is too high given my impulsive tendency to procrastinate, but going out now fixes the immediate relational tension.

goals.description: Spend exactly 45 minutes reviewing my notes on the hardest concepts only, then stop immediately.
goals.rationale: I have the knowledge to pass this, so pushing myself to exhaustion right now would just make me feel like a failure afterward, which is counterproductive. Short bursts work better than marathon sessions when I'm running on fumes. If I finish this in 45 mins, I'll be rested enough to handle the club night tomorrow; if not, I'll drop it entirely rather than burning out completely.

desires: Get out of this room with Mina and Jae so I don't have to explain why I'm ignoring them again.
Finish my midterm prep tonight before the deadline hits, but only if I can do it without feeling like a total failure afterward.
Not let anyone see that I'm running on fumes from sleep debt while trying to be the 'reliable' one for everyone else.
